revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''大久米命'''（おおくめ の みこと）とは、『[[記紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[久米氏|久米直]]（くめ…」
revision.text:
'''大久米命'''（おおくめ の みこと）とは、『[[記紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[久米氏|久米直]]（くめのあたい）の祖として伝えられている[[豪族]]。『[[日本書紀]]』では「大来目」と表記されている。
== 系譜 == 『[[古事記]]』によると、[[神武天皇]]の東征に[[大伴氏]]の先祖、[[道臣命]]（日臣命）と共に従軍し、[[大和国]]（今の[[奈良県]]）の[[宇陀]]（うだ）の豪族[[兄猾|兄宇迦斯]]（えうかし）や、忍坂（おさか、現在の[[桜井市]]の忍坂）の [[土蜘蛛|土雲]] （つちぐも）[[八十梟帥|八十建]]（やそたける）を討ち<ref name="jimmu">『古事記』中巻、神武天皇条</ref>大和を平定したとされる。兄猾討伐についての記述は『日本書紀』巻第三にもあり<ref>『日本書紀』神武天皇即位前紀戊午年10月1日条</ref>、戦闘の際には兵士の士気を鼓舞する目的で戦闘歌、[[久米歌]]が歌われたという。
『記紀』の [[天孫降臨]]にまつわる記述には、大久米命の祖先は『古事記』では [[天久米命|天津久米命]]（あまつくめのみこと）<ref>『古事記』上巻 天孫邇邇芸命条</ref>、『書紀』巻第二の第四の書では天槵津大来目（あめくしつおおくめ）<ref>『日本書紀』神代下第九段</ref>となっている。いずれの場合も天孫・天皇の警護、戦闘にまつわる職務に従事しているという点で共通している。
『古事記』における大久米命と道臣命は対等の立場で神武軍を指揮しているが、『書紀』では道臣命が久米部に司令しているかのように描かれている。このことは天孫降臨の項目でも同様になっており、このことから、久米部を指導する一族が没落し、その後、大伴氏が久米部を管轄するようになった、とも考えられる。
『古事記』では、大久米命は結婚前の神武天皇の皇后[[伊須気余理比売]]に歌で呼びかけ、天皇との媒介をつとめている。またその中で歌われた[[旋頭歌]]では、「黥（さ）ける利目（とめ）」とあり、目のまわりに入れ墨をしていたらしい<ref name="jimmu"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『古事記』完訳日本の古典1、[[小学館]]、1983年 * 『日本書紀』（一）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年
== 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[神武天皇]] * [[神武東征]] * [[道臣命]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:久米氏|おおくめのみこと]]

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『古事記』完訳日本の古典1、[[小学館]]、1983年 * 『日本書紀』（一）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年
== 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[神武天皇]] * [[神武東征]] * [[道臣命]]

revision.text:
'''大久米命'''（おおくめ の みこと）とは、『[[記紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[久米氏|久米直]]（くめのあたい）の祖として伝えられている[[豪族]]。『[[日本書紀]]』では「大来目」と表記されている。
== 系譜 == 『[[古事記]]』によると、[[神武天皇]]の東征に[[大伴氏]]の先祖、[[道臣命]]（日臣命）と共に従軍し、[[大和国]]（今の[[奈良県]]）の[[宇陀]]（うだ）の豪族[[兄猾|兄宇迦斯]]（えうかし）や、忍坂（おさか、現在の[[桜井市]]の忍坂）の [[土蜘蛛|土雲]] （つちぐも）[[八十梟帥|八十建]]（やそたける）を討ち<ref name="jimmu">『古事記』中巻、神武天皇条</ref>、大和を平定したとされる。兄猾討伐についての記述は『日本書紀』巻第三にもあり<ref>『日本書紀』神武天皇即位前紀戊午年10月2日条</ref>、戦闘の際には兵士の士気を鼓舞する目的で戦闘歌、[[久米歌]]が歌われたという。
『記紀』の [[天孫降臨]]にまつわる記述には、大久米命の祖先は『古事記』では [[天久米命|天津久米命]]（あまつくめのみこと）<ref>『古事記』上巻 天孫邇邇芸命条</ref>、『書紀』巻第二の第四の書では天槵津大来目（あめくしつおおくめ）<ref>『日本書紀』神代下第九段</ref>となっている。いずれの場合も天孫・天皇の警護、戦闘にまつわる職務に従事しているという点で共通している。
『古事記』における大久米命と道臣命は対等の立場で神武軍を指揮しているが、『書紀』では道臣命が[[久米部]]に司令しているかのように描かれている。天孫降臨の項目でも同様になっており、このことから、久米部を指導する一族が没落し、その後、大伴氏が久米部を管轄するようになった、とも考えられる。
『古事記』では、大久米命は結婚前の神武天皇の皇后[[伊須気余理比売]]に歌で呼びかけ、天皇との媒介をつとめている。またその中で歌われた[[旋頭歌]]では、「黥（さ）ける利目（とめ）」とあり、目のまわりに入れ墨をしていたとされる<ref name="jimmu"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『古事記』完訳日本の古典1、[[小学館]]、1983年 * 『日本書紀』（一）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史1 神話から歴史へ』、[[井上光貞]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 別冊歴史読本「謎の歴史書『古事記』『日本書紀』」歴史の謎シリーズ6、より「古代天皇の謎と問題点」p186 - p187、文：[[小林敏男 (歴史学者)|小林敏男]]、[[新人物往来社]]、1986年
== 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[神武天皇]] * [[神武東征]] * [[道臣命]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおくめ の みこと}} [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:久米氏|おおくめのみこと]]

『古事記』では、大久米命は結婚前の神武天皇の皇后[[伊須気余理比売]]に歌で呼びかけ、天皇との媒介をつとめている。またその中で歌われた[[旋頭歌]]では、「黥（さ）ける利目（とめ）」とあり、目のまわりに入れ墨をしていたとされる<ref name="jimmu"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『古事記』完訳日本の古典1、[[小学館]]、1983年 * 『日本書紀』（一）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史1 神話から歴史へ』、[[井上光貞]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 別冊歴史読本「謎の歴史書『古事記』『日本書紀』」歴史の謎シリーズ6、より「古代天皇の謎と問題点」p186 - p187、文：[[小林敏男 (歴史学者)|小林敏男]]、[[新人物往来社]]、1986年

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大久米命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[上古]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大来目 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[久米氏|久米直]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[天多祁箇命]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[布理禰命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''大久米命'''（おおくめ の みこと）とは、『[[記紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[久米氏|久米直]]（くめのあたい）の祖として伝えられている[[豪族]]。『[[日本書紀]]』では「大来目」と表記されている。
== 系譜 == 『[[古事記]]』によると、[[神武天皇]]の東征に[[大伴氏]]の先祖、[[道臣命]]（日臣命）と共に従軍し、[[大和国]]（今の[[奈良県]]）の[[宇陀]]（うだ）の豪族[[兄猾|兄宇迦斯]]（えうかし）や、忍坂（おさか、現在の[[桜井市]]の忍坂）の [[土蜘蛛|土雲]] （つちぐも）[[八十梟帥|八十建]]（やそたける）を討ち<ref name="jimmu">『古事記』中巻、神武天皇条</ref>、大和を平定したとされる。兄猾討伐についての記述は『日本書紀』巻第三にもあり<ref>『日本書紀』神武天皇即位前紀戊午年10月2日条</ref>、戦闘の際には兵士の士気を鼓舞する目的で戦闘歌、[[久米歌]]が歌われたという。
『記紀』の [[天孫降臨]]にまつわる記述には、大久米命の祖先は『古事記』では [[天津久米命]]（あまつくめのみこと）<ref>『古事記』上巻 天孫邇邇芸命条</ref>、『書紀』巻第二の第四の書では天槵津大来目（あめくしつおおくめ）<ref>『日本書紀』神代下第九段</ref>となっている。いずれの場合も天孫・天皇の警護、戦闘にまつわる職務に従事しているという点で共通している。
『古事記』における大久米命と道臣命は対等の立場で神武軍を指揮しているが、『書紀』では道臣命が[[久米部]]に司令しているかのように描かれている。天孫降臨の項目でも同様になっており、このことから、久米部を指導する一族が没落し、その後、大伴氏が久米部を管轄するようになった、とも考えられる。
『古事記』では、大久米命は結婚前の神武天皇の皇后[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|伊須気余理比売]]に歌で呼びかけ、天皇との媒介をつとめている。またその中で歌われた[[旋頭歌]]では、「黥（さ）ける利目（とめ）」とあり、目のまわりに入れ墨をしていたとされる<ref name="jimmu"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『古事記』完訳日本の古典1、[[小学館]]、1983年 * 『日本書紀』（一）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史1 神話から歴史へ』、[[井上光貞]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 別冊歴史読本「謎の歴史書『古事記』『日本書紀』」歴史の謎シリーズ6、より「古代天皇の謎と問題点」p186 - p187、文：[[小林敏男 (歴史学者)|小林敏男]]、[[新人物往来社]]、1986年
== 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[神武天皇]] * [[神武東征]] * [[道臣命]] * [[久米御縣神社]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|lt=Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|ja|土蜘蛛}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥}}, and other opponents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amatsukume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命}},<ref>''Kojiki'', upper volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == '' ''Kojiki'' (Complete Translation), Shōgakukan, 1983. '' ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volume 1), Iwanami Bunko, 1994. '' Mitsusada, Inoue. ''Myth to History'', Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. '' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986.
== Related Topics ==
* '' {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米氏}}'' '' {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}'' * ''{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}'' * ''{{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|lt=Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}'' * ''{{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命}}'' * ''{{ill|Kume Miyatake Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社}}'' * ''{{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|lt=Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|ja|土蜘蛛}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥}}, and other opponents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amatsukume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命}},<ref>''Kojiki'', upper volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == '' ''Kojiki'' (Complete Translation), Shōgakukan, 1983. '' ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volume 1), Iwanami Bunko, 1994. '' Mitsusada, Inoue. ''Myth to History'', Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. '' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986.

The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|lt=Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|ja|土蜘蛛}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥}}, and other opponents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amatsukume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命}},<ref>''Kojiki'', upper volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|lt=Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|ja|土蜘蛛}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥}}, and other opponents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amatsukume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命}},<ref>''Kojiki'', upper volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == '' ''Kojiki'' (Complete Translation), Shōgakukan, 1983. '' ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volume 1), Iwanami Bunko, 1994. '' Mitsusada, Inoue. ''Myth to History'', Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. '' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986.
== Related Topics ==
* '' {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米氏}}'' '' {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}'' * ''{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}'' * ''{{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|lt=Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}'' * ''{{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命}}'' * ''{{ill|Kume Miyatake Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社}}'' * ''{{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|lt=Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|ja|土蜘蛛}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥}}, and other opponents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amatsukume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命}},<ref>''Kojiki'', upper volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == '' ''Kojiki'' (Complete Translation), Shōgakukan, 1983. '' ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volume 1), Iwanami Bunko, 1994. '' Mitsusada, Inoue. ''Myth to History'', Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. '' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986.

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大久米命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[上古]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大来目 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[久米氏|久米直]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[天多祁箇命]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[布理禰命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''大久米命'''（おおくめ の みこと）とは、『[[記紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[久米氏|久米直]]（くめのあたい）の祖として伝えられている[[豪族]]。『[[日本書紀]]』では「大来目」と表記されている。
== 系譜 == 『[[古事記]]』によると、[[神武天皇]]の東征に[[大伴氏]]の先祖、[[道臣命]]（日臣命）と共に従軍し、[[大和国]]（今の[[奈良県]]）の[[宇陀]]（うだ）の豪族[[兄猾|兄宇迦斯]]（えうかし）や、忍坂（おさか、現在の[[桜井市]]の忍坂）の [[土蜘蛛|土雲]] （つちぐも）[[八十梟帥|八十建]]（やそたける）を討ち<ref name="jimmu">『古事記』中巻、神武天皇条</ref>、大和を平定したとされる。兄猾討伐についての記述は『日本書紀』巻第三にもあり<ref>『日本書紀』神武天皇即位前紀戊午年10月2日条</ref>、戦闘の際には兵士の士気を鼓舞する目的で戦闘歌、[[久米歌]]が歌われたという。
『記紀』の [[天孫降臨]]にまつわる記述には、大久米命の祖先は『古事記』では [[天津久米命]]（あまつくめのみこと）<ref>『古事記』上巻 天孫邇邇芸命条</ref>、『書紀』巻第二の第四の書では天槵津大来目（あめくしつおおくめ）<ref>『日本書紀』神代下第九段</ref>となっている。いずれの場合も天孫・天皇の警護、戦闘にまつわる職務に従事しているという点で共通している。
『古事記』における大久米命と道臣命は対等の立場で神武軍を指揮しているが、『書紀』では道臣命が[[久米部]]に司令しているかのように描かれている。天孫降臨の項目でも同様になっており、このことから、久米部を指導する一族が没落し、その後、大伴氏が久米部を管轄するようになった、とも考えられる。
『古事記』では、大久米命は結婚前の神武天皇の皇后[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|伊須気余理比売]]に歌で呼びかけ、天皇との媒介をつとめている。またその中で歌われた[[旋頭歌]]では、「黥（さ）ける利目（とめ）」とあり、目のまわりに入れ墨をしていたとされる<ref name="jimmu"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『古事記』完訳日本の古典1、[[小学館]]、1983年 * 『日本書紀』（一）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史1 神話から歴史へ』、[[井上光貞]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 別冊歴史読本「謎の歴史書『古事記』『日本書紀』」歴史の謎シリーズ6、より「古代天皇の謎と問題点」p186 - p187、文：[[小林敏男 (歴史学者)|小林敏男]]、[[新人物往来社]]、1986年
== 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[神武天皇]] * [[神武東征]] * [[道臣命]] * [[久米御縣神社]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]

== 系譜 == 『[[古事記]]』によると、[[神武天皇]]の東征に[[大伴氏]]の先祖、[[道臣命]]（日臣命）と共に従軍し、[[大和国]]（今の[[奈良県]]）の[[宇陀]]（うだ）の豪族[[兄猾|兄宇迦斯]]（えうかし）や、忍坂（おさか、現在の[[桜井市]]の忍坂）の [[土蜘蛛|土雲]] （つちぐも）[[八十梟帥|八十建]]（やそたける）を討ち<ref name="jimmu">『古事記』中巻、神武天皇条</ref>、大和を平定したとされる。兄猾討伐についての記述は『日本書紀』巻第三にもあり<ref>『日本書紀』神武天皇即位前紀戊午年10月2日条</ref>、戦闘の際には兵士の士気を鼓舞する目的で戦闘歌、[[久米歌]]が歌われたという。
『記紀』の [[天孫降臨]]にまつわる記述には、大久米命の祖先は『古事記』では [[天津久米命]]（あまつくめのみこと）<ref>『古事記』上巻 天孫邇邇芸命条</ref>、『書紀』巻第二の第四の書では天槵津大来目（あめくしつおおくめ）<ref>『日本書紀』神代下第九段</ref>となっている。いずれの場合も天孫・天皇の警護、戦闘にまつわる職務に従事しているという点で共通している。
『古事記』における大久米命と道臣命は対等の立場で神武軍を指揮しているが、『書紀』では道臣命が[[久米部]]に司令しているかのように描かれている。天孫降臨の項目でも同様になっており、このことから、久米部を指導する一族が没落し、その後、大伴氏が久米部を管轄するようになった、とも考えられる。
『古事記』では、大久米命は結婚前の神武天皇の皇后[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|伊須気余理比売]]に歌で呼びかけ、天皇との媒介をつとめている。またその中で歌われた[[旋頭歌]]では、「黥（さ）ける利目（とめ）」とあり、目のまわりに入れ墨をしていたとされる<ref name="jimmu"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『古事記』完訳日本の古典1、[[小学館]]、1983年 * 『日本書紀』（一）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史1 神話から歴史へ』、[[井上光貞]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 別冊歴史読本「謎の歴史書『古事記』『日本書紀』」歴史の謎シリーズ6、より「古代天皇の謎と問題点」p186 - p187、文：[[小林敏男 (歴史学者)|小林敏男]]、[[新人物往来社]]、1986年
== 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[神武天皇]] * [[神武東征]] * [[道臣命]] * [[久米御縣神社]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55529547]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotakikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furine no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], recorded in texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]] of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tsuchigumo|Tsuchigumo]] lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|qq=|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|qq=|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == '' ''Kojiki'' (Complete Translation), Shōgakukan, 1983. '' ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volume 1), Iwanami Bunko, 1994. '' Mitsusada, Inoue. ''Myth to History'', Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. '' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米氏|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]] * {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi}} * {{ill|Kume no Miagata Shrine|lt=Kume Miyatake Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Miyatake Shrine}} * {{ill|List of ancient Japanese figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}
{{translated page|ja|大久米命|version=98221649|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55529547]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotakikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furine no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], recorded in texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]] of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tsuchigumo|Tsuchigumo]] lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tsuchigumo|Tsuchigumo]] lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55529547]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotakikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furine no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], recorded in texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]] of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tsuchigumo|Tsuchigumo]] lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes ==

'' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986. == See Also ==

The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], recorded in texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]] of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tsuchigumo|Tsuchigumo]] lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55529547]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotakikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furine no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], recorded in texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]] of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tsuchigumo|Tsuchigumo]] lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}

'' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986. == See Also ==

Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大久米命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55529547]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotakikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furine no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], recorded in texts such as the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]] of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tsuchigumo|Tsuchigumo]] lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}

'' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986. == See Also ==

== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|en|Tsuchigumo|WD=Q2063089|lt=Tsuchigumo}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes ==

{{Infobox noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotakikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furine no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|en|Tsuchigumo|WD=Q2063089|lt=Tsuchigumo|1=Tsuchigumo}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />

{{Infobox noble | name = Ōkume no Mikoto | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, {{ill|Kofun period|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōkume | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Amanotakikami|ja|天多祁箇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotakikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Furine no Mikoto|ja|布理禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furine no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_works = }}
'''Ōkume no Mikoto''' (大久米命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. He is revered as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} clan, a prominent ancient warrior lineage. The ''Nihon Shoki'' sometimes refers to him as "Ōkume" (大来目).
== Genealogy == According to the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto joined the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, alongside {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}). Together, they defeated local chieftains such as {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tsuchigumo|en|Tsuchigumo|WD=Q2063089|lt=Tsuchigumo|1=Tsuchigumo}} lord {{ill|Yasotakeru|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasotakeru|qq=}}, and other o11onents in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.<ref name="jimmu">''Kojiki'', middle volume, Emperor Jimmu section.</ref> His actions are celebrated in the ''Nihon Shoki'', which notes the singing of {{ill|Kume no Uta|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Uta|qq=}}, martial songs to inspire warriors during battle.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Emperor Jimmu pre-enthronement, year戊午, 10th month, 2nd day.</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' identifies Ōkume no Mikoto’s ancestor as {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天津久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukume no Mikoto|qq=}},<ref>''Kojiki'', u11er volume, Tenson Ninigi section.</ref> while the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ame Kushitsu Ōkume" (天槵津大来目). Both sources agree on their role as protectors of the {{ill|Heavenly Grandchild|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Grandchild|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}, indicating a long-standing military tradition.
== Legacy == In the ''Kojiki'', Ōkume no Mikoto is depicted as an equal partner to Michinoomi no Mikoto in leading Emperor Jimmu's forces. However, in the ''Nihon Shoki'', Ōkume is portrayed as subordinate, suggesting that the Kumebe clan's influence declined over time, with leadership passing to the Ōtomo clan.
The ''Kojiki'' also records Ōkume’s role in arranging Emperor Jimmu’s marriage to {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|伊須気余理比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}. During the courtship, Ōkume performed a {{ill|Sedōka|ja|旋頭歌|fr|Sedōka|it|Sedōka|hu|Szedóka|pl|Sedōka|hi|सेदोका|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sedōka|1=sedōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q678489}} that refers to "tattooed eyes," implying he may have worn traditional warrior tattoos.<ref name="jimmu" />
== Notes ==

== References == '' ''Kojiki'' (Complete Translation), Shōgakukan, 1983. '' ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volume 1), Iwanami Bunko, 1994. '' Mitsusada, Inoue. ''Myth to History'', Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. '' Kobayashi Toshio, "Mysteries of the Ancient Japanese Emperors," ''History Mysteries Series 6'', Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha, 1986. == See Also ==
